title: Indulgent Crispy Peruvian Pepper Paella
description: Fatty, crispy weeknight Peruvian dish featuring sweet red peppers, sous‑vide, jain-friendly, served in a paella pan.
instructions: In a vacuum bag combine rice, vegetable stock, sliced peppers, corn, olives, capers, lemon zest, thyme, and a pinch of salt; seal the bag.
Submerge the bag in the preheated water bath and cook for 1 hour and 30 minutes.
Remove the bag, drain the liquid, and let the rice mixture cool slightly for 5 minutes.
Heat a paella pan over medium heat and add 1 tablespoon of peanut oil, swirling to coat the bottom.
Add the cooled rice mixture to the pan and stir to distribute the oil evenly, cooking for 5 minutes until the surface begins to brown.
Increase heat to high and pour the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil, allowing the mixture to sit undisturbed for 2 minutes to develop a crispy crust.
Flip the paella gently with a spatula and sear the other side for 1 minute, ensuring a crunchy exterior.
Serve hot garnished with fresh thyme leaves and a squeeze of lime if desired.
